title: arisarum vulgare var. subexertum
intro: Arisarum vulgare var. subexertum is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Arum lily, a flowering plant that belongs to the Arum family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Arisarum vulgare var. subexertum include its potential to aid in respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
